task: I’m putting together a small house reference sheet for the kitchen drawer before winter, so I don’t have to hunt for things if something comes up. Could you help me figure out which details are worth keeping on one page and a clean way to arrange them?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — for a one-page kitchen drawer sheet, I’d keep it **plain, fast to scan, and limited to the things you’d actually need in a hurry**.

## What’s worth keeping
I’d group it into 3 short sections:

### 1) Emergency / “if something goes wrong”
- Name and address
- Your phone number
- One emergency contact
- Who to call first if you can’t handle it yourself
- Shutoff locations:
  - water
  - gas, if you have it
  - electric / breaker panel
- Insurance claims number

### 2) Repair / service numbers
- Plumber
- Electrician
- HVAC / furnace service
- Appliance repair, only if you actually use one
- Any other one or two people you call most often

### 3) Reference notes
A small notes line for:
- filter sizes
- bulb types
- battery sizes
- breaker panel notes
- model numbers
- anything you keep buying again

You could also add:
- date last checked
- where the paper copy of warranties/manuals is, if you keep one

## Clean one-page layout
Here’s a simple order that stays easy to read:

**HOUSE REFERENCE**  
Name:  
Address:  
My phone:  

**EMERGENCY**  
Call first:  
Emergency contact:  
Water shutoff:  
Gas shutoff:  
Electric/breaker panel:  
Insurance claims:  

**REPAIR CONTACTS**  
Plumber:  
Electrician:  
HVAC/Furnace:  
Appliance repair:  

**NOTES**  
Filter size / bulb type / battery size / model numbers:  

**LAST CHECKED**  
Date:  

## A few small design tips
- Keep it to **one side of one page** if possible
- Use **big labels** and leave room to handwrite numbers
- Put the most urgent items near the top
- Don’t add anything you wouldn’t actually use
- If it starts feeling crowded, cut it back

If you want, I can turn this into a **very plain fill-in template** you could copy by hand.

user_simulator.text:
Pretty much the right idea. I’d want it a little tighter, though. Too much on one page and it turns into clutter, which defeats the point.

I’d keep only what I’d need fast:
- name, address, phone
- one emergency contact
- water shutoff
- breaker panel location
- furnace/HVAC service
- plumber
- maybe one electrician
- insurance claims number
- filter size and any battery sizes I actually use
- date last checked

I’d skip the extra filler unless I know I’m going to use it. Appliance repair, model numbers, that sort of thing — only if there’s room and it matters.

Could you lay it out as a plain template, very simple, with maybe 3 blocks max? I don’t need anything fancy.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter, plain 3-block version you could copy by hand:

---

**HOUSE REFERENCE**

**1) BASIC INFO**  
Name: ____________________  
Address: __________________  
Phone: ____________________  
Emergency contact: ____________________

**2) QUICK NEED-TO-KNOW**  
Water shutoff: ____________________  
Breaker panel location: ____________________  
Furnace / HVAC service: ____________________  
Plumber: ____________________  
Electrician: ____________________  
Insurance claims: ____________________

**3) NOTES / CHECKED**  
Filter size(s): ____________________  
Battery size(s): ____________________  
Date last checked: ____________________

---

If you want, I can make this even more compact so it fits on a half page or looks better for handwriting.

user_simulator.text:
Yep. That’s the shape of it. Clean enough to use, not crammed full of junk.

I’d probably write it just about like that and leave a little extra room for the things I may need to add later. The 3 blocks make sense.
